notes: This lane cleaned checked-in live-shadow workflow guidance rather than historical archived evidence.

A second follow-up swept adjacent checked-in Ray reviewer evidence after discovering stale inline-Python entrypoints in ray-live-jobs.json and mixed-workload-matrix-report.json.
A third follow-up removed Python-specific wording from the active Symphony bootstrap template and locked it with the root residual-sweep regression guard.
A fourth follow-up normalized the active refill queue planning guidance to use Go-first legacy wording instead of Python-specific path language.
A fifth follow-up normalized the maintained legacy-mainline compatibility manifest so it uses generic legacy runtime wording instead of Python-specific mainline phrasing.
A sixth follow-up tightened active Go CLI migration guidance so current-state replacement sections use generic legacy-script wording instead of unnecessary Python-specific phrasing.
A seventh follow-up tightened the root Go CLI migration plan so current-state replacement bullets use generic legacy-script wording instead of unnecessary Python-specific phrasing.

A ninth follow-up tightened active migration planning and PR-suggestion guidance so current-state planning lines use generic legacy-script wording instead of unnecessary Python-specific phrasing.
A tenth follow-up tightened active migration-doc compatibility and risk guidance so current-state sections use generic legacy-script wording instead of unnecessary Python-specific phrasing.
An eleventh follow-up tightened the last current-state migration baseline sentence so it uses generic legacy-script wording instead of unnecessary Python-free phrasing.
